title: Pentameris epilis
taxonomy: Pentameris epilis belongs to the family Poaceae, which is commonly known as the grass family. Within the Poaceae family, Pentameris epilis is classified as a type of grass. The specific classification of Pentameris epilis is still a subject of ongoing research and may be subject to change as new information becomes available. As a member of the Poaceae family, Pentameris epilis shares characteristics with other grasses, such as long stems and flat, overlapping leaves. Further classification may involve identifying the specific genus and species within the Poaceae family.

intro: Pentameris epilis, also known as Bent grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of North America. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and is traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Pentameris epilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to create a decoction, which is used to soothe respiratory tract irritations and promote overall well-being.
